questionHtml:
Huntington’s disease is an autosomal dominant genetic disease. What are the chances of two parents that are heterozygous for the gene having a child with Huntington’s disease?

A. 25 %

B. 50 %

C. 75 %

D. 100 %

meta.title: Huntington’s disease is an autosomal dominant genetic disease. What are the chances of two parents that are heterozygous for the gene having a child with Huntington’s disease? A. 2
